channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Blues edge Penguins 5-4 in OT, running win streak to franchise-tying 11 games
item.description: Robert Thomas scored a power-play goal in overtime and the St. Louis Blues tied a franchise record with their 11th consecutive win, 5-4 over the Pittsburgh Penguins.
item.dc:creator: By Joe Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Morant beats the buzzer for a game-winner, lifts Grizzlies past Heat 110-108
item.description: Ja Morant made a 12-foot jumper in the lane as time expired, lifting the Memphis Grizzlies to a 110-108 win over the Miami Heat and giving interim coach Tuomas Iisalo his first victory with the team.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Giannis' NBA 1st of 35 points, 20 assists, 17 rebounds sends Bucks past 76ers 126-113
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo became the first player in NBA history to have 35 points, 17 rebounds and a career-high 20 assists as the Milwaukee Bucks beat the Philadelphia 76ers 126-113.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dodgers pitcher Blake Snell says home was broken into shortly before his start in home opener
item.description: Blake Snell said in a statement that his Seattle-area home was broken into just before he started in the Los Angeles Dodgers' home opener.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sam Ryder takes only 21 putts for a 63 and leads Texas Open
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sam Ryder wasn't sure his neck would feel good enough for him to play Thursday in the Valero Texas Open. He found a swing and hardly missed a putt on his way to a 9-under 63 and a one-shot lead over Keith Mitchell in the final tournament before the Masters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Sox beneift the most from the new dimensions in Baltimore in Camden Yards' first series of 2025
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — So far, it's the opposition taking advantage of the latest change in dimensions at Camden Yards. The Boston Red Sox hit three two-run home runs Thursday in an 8-4 win over Baltimore, and all of them went to left field, where the Orioles moved the wall in a bit during the offseason.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alex Ovechkin scores career goal No 892 as he nears Wayne Gretzky's all-time mark
item.description: Washington Capitals star Alex Ovechkin inched closer to breaking Wayne Gretzky's career goals record on Wednesday night with a score against the Carolina Hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club brought home Gold in Las Vegas
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Once high school volleyball season ends, several local high school athletes from all across the Western Slope come together to compete at the club level for the Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, WGN 9

item.title: GJ Tigers Girls Soccer pours it on Central 10-0
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — There was no moment to take a breath at Unity Field Tuesday night. Seemingly every few seconds yielded a breakaway chance for the Grand Junction Tigers' aggressive attack. And many of those chances were cashed in by the Tigers. Ten of them.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years in 18-3 rout of A's in West Sacramento
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Milwaukee pays tribute to Bob Uecker in Brewers' first home game since his death
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — As the Milwaukee Brewers conducted a moment of silence before their home opener to honor Bob Uecker, one fan decided keeping quiet wasn’t the best way to pay tribute to the lovable loquacious broadcaster. “We love you, Ueck,” the spectator shouted.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NFL owners meetings kick off in South Florida
item.description: With free agency still in full swing and the NFL Draft less than a month away, NFL owners, head coaches, and front office executives have made their way to The Breakers in Palm Beach for the league's annual owners meetings.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utes lose to Butler in College Basktball Crown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - The Runnin' Utes got one more opportunity to play this season, but lost to Butler in the inaugural College Basketball Crown tournament in Las Vegas Monday, 86-84. Keanu Dawes, who entered the transfer portal last week, led the Utes with 19 points and 11 rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Padres sweep Braves, 4-0 to begin a season for the first time since 1984
item.description: Break out the broomsticks! The San Diego Padres sweep the Atlanta Braves to begin their 2025 MLB season.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego Padres sweep Atlanta Braves in 2025 opening series
item.description: The San Diego Padres secured a four-game sweep of the Atlanta Braves with a 5-0 win Sunday, marking their second time in franchise history to start a season 4-0, with Nick Pivetta making a dominant debut.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Kyren Paris hits solo home run after long weather delay to lift Angels over White Sox 3-2
item.description: Kyren Paris homered on the first pitch he saw after a lengthy rain delay and the Los Angeles Angels rallied to beat the Chicago White Sox 3-2 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lauren Betts and UCLA reach first Final Four of women's NCAA Tournament, beating LSU 72-65
item.description: SPOKANE, Wash. (AP) — Lauren Betts had 17 points and seven rebounds despite spending the entire second quarter on the bench, and top overall seed UCLA reached its first Final Four of the women's NCAA Tournament with a 72-65 victory over LSU on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL gets shut out again at Minnesota United, 2-0
item.description: ST. PAUL (ABC4 Sports) - For the second straight game, and third overall this MLS season, Real Salt Lake failed to score a goal in a 2-0 shutout at Minnesota United Saturday night. Tani Oluwaseyi recorded a brace to help Minnesota United hand Real Salt Lake its fourth loss of the season. Dayne St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Kyle Tucker homers as the Cubs hold off the Diamondbacks for a 4-3 win
item.description: Kyle Tucker hit his first home run with the Chicago Cubs, leading his new team to a 4-3 victory over the Arizona Diamondbacks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CF Montreal seeks first win of the season, visits the Chicago Fire
item.description: CF Montreal (0-4-1) vs. Chicago Fire (3-1-1) Chicago; Saturday, 8:30 p.m. EDT BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Chicago -175, Montreal +429, Draw +308; over/under is 2.5 goals BOTTOM LINE: CF Montreal seeks its first victory of the season when it visits the Chicago Fire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Giddey's half-court buzzer beater completes miraculous comeback against Lakers, 119-117
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Josh Giddey could feel it as soon as the ball left his fingertips. In a flash, teammates were mobbing him.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
